heading: Assistant Secretary of Commerce; appointment; compensation; duties
label: 1507b Assistant Secretary of Commerce; appointment; compensation; duties
text: There shall be in the Department of Commerce, in addition to the Assistant Secretaries provided by law as of , one additional Assistant Secretary of Commerce who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate. Such Assistant Secretary shall perform such duties as the Secretary of Commerce shall prescribe.November 12, 1977
notes: Amendments1982— substituted “Such Assistant Secretary shall perform such duties” for “Such Assistant Secretary shall receive compensation at the rate prescribed by law for Assistant Secretaries of Commerce, and shall perform such duties”.Pub. L. 97–195
amendments: Amendments1982— substituted “Such Assistant Secretary shall perform such duties” for “Such Assistant Secretary shall receive compensation at the rate prescribed by law for Assistant Secretaries of Commerce, and shall perform such duties”.Pub. L. 97–195
